alerts.title: Iran-linked hackers claim compromise of FBI drone systems; threaten World Cup targeting
alerts.summary: Iran-linked cyber group publicly claimed successful hack of FBI unmanned aerial systems and issued threats targeting World Cup infrastructure, per global_metrics cyber headlines (France 24, CBS News). Represents direct escalation of Iranian cyber operations against US critical infrastructure amid active US-Iran deal negotiations.

alerts.title: Israeli strikes kill 3 in south Lebanon; Trump condemns Netanyahu, warns deal imperiled
alerts.summary: Israel conducted multiple airstrikes in southern Beirut suburbs killing at least 3 civilians following US-Iran ceasefire announcement (Reuters, NBC News, incidents feed). Trump publicly criticized Netanyahu's actions as "too much" and stated he is "not happy" with Israel's Hezbollah strategy, signaling acute US-Israel friction over de-escalation commitments.

alerts.title: CIA director warns Trump: Iran will not honor nuclear commitments despite deal framework
alerts.summary: CIA Director John Ratcliffe briefed President Trump and senior officials that US intelligence assesses Iran does not intend to comply with nuclear obligations under a final agreement (Axios, telegram feed from Middle East Spectator, ~40.5K views). Undermines diplomatic breakthrough and raises breakout-timeline risk despite ongoing framework talks.

alerts.title: Three US carrier strike groups maintain forward deployment across Red Sea, Arabian Sea, Mediterranean
alerts.summary: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (Red Sea), USS Abraham Lincoln (Arabian Sea), and USS Gerald R. Ford (Mediterranean) remain deployed across critical regional chokepoints (assets feed). Positioning consistent with force protection and freedom-of-navigation enforcement amid elevated proxy threat levels and ongoing Israeli-Hezbollah exchanges.
alerts.region: Strait of Hormuz, Red Sea, Arabian Sea, Mediterranean

alerts.title: US media sentiment remains cautiously positive on Trump-Iran deal despite escalation signals
alerts.summary: US sentiment analysis shows 52% positive vs. 39% negative coverage (up from previous baseline), with "trump," "iran," and "deal" as top trending terms; however, word-frequency spike in "war" (11 mentions) reflects ongoing Israeli strikes and nuclear verification disputes (sentiment feed). Indicates public discourse still favors diplomatic framework but with persistent undercurrent of conflict concern.

analysis_note: US-Iran ceasefire framework holds formally but faces dual-track erosion: Israeli strikes threaten implementation while Iranian cyber-escalation and CIA warnings on nuclear non-compliance undermine US-Iran trust; threat level remains CRITICAL (9/10) with proxy forces elevated and nuclear breakout window unchanged at 1-2 weeks.
